channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why CX is every brand’s biggest opportunity
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: John Sabino, Fast Company

item.title: Should NBN 50 be the cheapest broadband speed tier? ACCAN certainly thinks so
item.description: ACCAN is calling on the NBN to scrap NBN 25 as its entry-level offering and replace it with cheaply accessible NBN 50.
item.dc:creator: zac.kelly@futurenet.com (Zachariah Kelly), TechRadar

item.title: New Orleans Lawsuit: Airbnb Challenges City's Regulations - A Detailed Analysis
item.description: Airbnb sues New Orleans over regulations requiring short-term rental platforms to ensure properties are compliant with city laws.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mass Stranding of False Killer Whales in Tasmania: A Tragic Event Unfolds
item.description: At least 90 false killer whales stranded on a remote beach in Australia's island state of Tasmania, with experts giving up hope of rescuing more than half of them due to unfavorable ocean and weather conditions.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: These fantastic sites will save you from ever getting a bad seat again
item.description: When you’re booking travel, scoring a ticket to a sporting event, or securing yourself a spot at some other sort of show, you’re frequently faced with the impossible-seeming task of committing to a specific seat—on the spot. It may seem simple.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: One Redditor spotted an AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D for $99 in the wildest price drop I've ever seen
item.description: It might be a bit of a head-scratcher at first, but yes, AMD's Ryzen 7 7800X3D was recently available for $99 at one retail store.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 has finally raced its way onto Paramount Plus, and I'm gutted that I won't be able to stream it
item.description: The third Sonic the Hedgehog movie has made its official streaming debut, but only in certain countries.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Musk’s xAI launches Grok 3, expanding AI capabilities with deep search and reasoning
item.description: Elon Musk’s AI startup xAI has introduced Grok 3, the latest version of its chatbot model, which Musk describes as the most advanced AI system yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Come costruire un team di successo e vincere la battaglia per i talenti
item.description: Avere un team IT ad alte prestazioni è sicuramente uno dei principali obiettivi dei Chief Information Officer. Riuscirci, ovviamente, dipende dalle persone che costituiscono la squadra di lavoro, ma la capacità del CIO di capire le sue risorse e comunicare la strategia è ugualmente importante.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple may be launching a new iPhone this week. It could sport a radical redesign
item.description: If you’re on the cusp of buying a new iPhone, you might want to hold off until this Wednesday, February 19. That’s the day Apple CEO Tim Cook says the company will be launching “the newest member of the family.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: How DoD modernization can advance mission readiness for the warfighter
item.description: Getting the right technology and data is imperative for driving DoD transformation initiatives, says Maximus’ Bryan Martz. The post How DoD modernization can advance mission readiness for the warfighter first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: Santorini earthquakes are decreasing but outlook is uncertain, top Greek scientist says
item.description: Greece’s chief seismologist says that while earthquake frequency around Santorini has slightly decreased, the situation remains unpredictable for the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Having fun with the Tech Interactive’s new AI-driven immersive exhibit
item.description: "Dream Garden," which opened to the public Saturday, uses artificial intelligence to let visitors shape the digital world around them.
item.dc:creator: Sal Pizarro, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Moss Landing battery fire: Residents sue Vistra, others for damages
item.description: A group of Monterey County residents filed a lawsuit in Alameda County against several companies for damages following the large fire at the Moss Landing Battery Plant on Jan. 16.
item.dc:creator: Kyarra Harris, The Mercury News

item.title: Arm secures Meta as first customer in chip push, challenging industry giants
item.description: In a landmark shift, Arm has secured Meta as the first major customer for its internally designed server CPUs, a move that signals its entry into direct chip sales and places it in direct competition with its biggest customers, including Qualcomm and Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Snowflake announces preview of Cortex Agent APIs to power enterprise data intelligence
item.description: Snowflake on Wednesday announced the public preview of Cortex Agents, a set of APIs built on top of the Snowflake Intelligence platform, a low-code offering that was first launched in November at Build, the company’s annual developer conference.
item.dc:creator: Info World
